tluaColonPredicateNarrowing.tlua(76,39): error TLUA2355: A function whose declared type is neither 'undefined', 'void', nor 'any' must return a value.
tluaColonPredicateNarrowing.tlua(83,46): error TLUA1225: Cannot find parameter 'missing'.
tluaColonPredicateNarrowing.tlua(99,14): error TLUA2554: Expected 1 arguments, but got 2.
tluaColonPredicateNarrowing.tlua(100,11): error TLUA2322: Type 'unknown' is not assignable to type 'Player'.
tluaColonPredicateNarrowing.tlua(104,23): error TLUA2339: Property 'Nick' does not exist on type 'Entity'.


==== tluaColonPredicateNarrowing.tlua (5 errors) ====
    // A colon call passes the receiver as the implicit first argument, so type
    // predicates on colon methods narrow through the shifted indices: declared
    // parameter 0 (`self is T`) narrows the receiver, and predicates on later
    // parameters narrow the corresponding written argument.
    
    interface Entity {
        IsPlayer(self: Entity): self is Player;
        AssertPlayer(self: Entity): asserts self is Player;
        Check(self: Entity, v: unknown): v is Player;
    }
    interface Player extends Entity {
        Nick(self: Player): string;
    }
    
    declare e: Entity;
    declare u: unknown;
    declare holder: { ent: Entity };
    
    // Receiver predicate narrows the receiver in the true branch; the false
    // branch keeps the base type.
    if e:IsPlayer() then
        local n: string = e:Nick();
    else
        local still: Entity = e;
    end
    
    // A predicate on a later parameter narrows the written argument (declared
    // index 1 is written argument 0).
    if e:Check(u) then
        local p: Player = u;
    end
    
    // Assertion predicates narrow the receiver for the rest of the flow.
    declare e2: Entity;
    e2:AssertPlayer();
    local an: string = e2:Nick();
    
    // Property-access receivers narrow too.
    if holder.ent:IsPlayer() then
        local hn: string = holder.ent:Nick();
    end
    
    // Plain-function predicates with a self-named parameter still narrow their
    // explicit argument (no index shift on dot calls).
    declare isP: (self: Entity) => self is Player;
    declare e3: Entity;
    if isP(e3) then
        local dn: string = e3:Nick();
    end
    
    // Typeless `asserts cond` predicates use the same colon-aware index mapping:
    // the assertion narrows the written argument at the shifted index, not the
    // one at the raw declared index.
    interface Guard {
        Ensure(self: Guard, cond: unknown): asserts cond;
        EnsureWith(self: Guard, cond: unknown, extra: unknown): asserts cond;
    }
    declare g: Guard;
    declare maybe: string | nil;
    g:Ensure(maybe ~= nil);
    local sure: string = maybe;
    
    // The predicate names `cond` (declared index 1 -> written argument 0); the
    // unrelated second argument must not be the one narrowed.
    declare maybe2: string | nil;
    declare unrelated: boolean;
    g:EnsureWith(maybe2 ~= nil, unrelated);
    local sure2: string = maybe2;
    
    // A colon-call assertion of a literal false marks the following code
    // unreachable: the missing-return error is suppressed after Ensure(false)
    // but still fires after Ensure(true).
    function neverReturns(gg: Guard): number
        gg:Ensure(false);
    end
    function stillNeedsReturn(gg: Guard): number
                                          ~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2355: A function whose declared type is neither 'undefined', 'void', nor 'any' must return a value.
        gg:Ensure(true);
    end
    
    // A predicate whose parameter name matches nothing narrows nothing on colon
    // calls either (the mismatch index stays out of range after the shift).
    interface Wrong {
        Bad(self: Wrong, cond: unknown): asserts missing;
                                                 ~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA1225: Cannot find parameter 'missing'.
    }
    declare w: Wrong;
    declare wv: string | nil;
    w:Bad(wv ~= nil);
    local wUnnarrowed: string | nil = wv;
    
    // Colon-calling a function-typed member with NO self parameter binds the
    // receiver to the predicate's declared parameter 0 (consistent with call
    // checking, which also reports the arity error) -- the written argument is
    // deliberately NOT narrowed.
    interface Holder2 {
        CheckV: (v: unknown) => v is Player;
    }
    declare h2: Holder2;
    declare u2: unknown;
    if h2:CheckV(u2) then
                 ~~
!!! error TLUA2554: Expected 1 arguments, but got 2.
        local notNarrowed: Player = u2;
              ~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'unknown' is not assignable to type 'Player'.
    end
    
    // Outside the guard the receiver is not narrowed.
    local bad: string = e:Nick();
                          ~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'Nick' does not exist on type 'Entity'.
